recommendations.title: Balanced Constitution Health Recommendations
recommendations.summary: Maintain your current good state, follow nature, nurture both body and spirit

content.name: Moderate eating
content.description: Regular timing and quantity, no overeating, no partial diet
content.descriptionEn: Regular timing and quantity, no overeating, no partial diet

content.name: Balanced five flavors
content.description: Balanced intake of sour, bitter, sweet, pungent, and salty
content.descriptionEn: Balanced intake of sour, bitter, sweet, pungent, and salty

content.name: Follow seasons
content.description: Nourish yang in spring/summer, yin in autumn/winter
content.descriptionEn: Nourish yang in spring/summer, yin in autumn/winter

sections.title: Daily Life Regulation
sections.content: Regular daily routine, balance work and rest, follow seasonal changes. Spring: late to bed, early to rise. Summer: late to bed, early to rise. Autumn: early to bed, early to rise. Winter: early to bed, late to rise.
sections.contentEn: Regular daily routine, balance work and rest, follow seasonal changes. Spring: late to bed, early to rise. Summer: late to bed, early to rise. Autumn: early to bed, early to rise. Winter: early to bed, late to rise.

content.name: Moderate exercise
content.description: Walking, jogging, swimming, etc.
content.descriptionEn: Walking, jogging, swimming, etc.

content.name: Traditional practices
content.description: Tai Chi, Baduanjin, Qigong
content.descriptionEn: Tai Chi, Baduanjin, Qigong

sections.title: Emotional Regulation
sections.content: Maintain peaceful mindset, smooth emotions, quiet emptiness, spirit kept internally. Avoid excessive emotional stimulation from joy, anger, worry, thought, sorrow, fear, and fright.
sections.contentEn: Maintain peaceful mindset, smooth emotions, quiet emptiness, spirit kept internally. Avoid excessive emotional stimulation from joy, anger, worry, thought, sorrow, fear, and fright.

recommendations.summaryEn: Maintain your current good state, follow nature, nurture both body and spirit

recommendations.title: Qi Deficiency Constitution Regulation
recommendations.summary: Boost Qi, strengthen spleen, avoid Qi depletion, supplement and benefit Qi

content.name: Eat Qi-boosting foods
content.description: Chinese yam, lotus seed, astragalus, codonopsis, white atractylodes, poria, jujube, longan, chicken, beef, carp, crucian carp
content.descriptionEn: Chinese yam, lotus seed, astragalus, codonopsis, white atractylodes, poria, jujube, longan, chicken, beef, carp, crucian carp

content.name: Avoid Qi-depleting foods
content.description: Raw radish, water spinach, hawthorn, betel nut - these foods deplete Qi
content.descriptionEn: Raw radish, water spinach, hawthorn, betel nut - these foods deplete Qi

content.name: Astragalus Chicken Broth
content.description: 30g Astragalus root, 1 whole chicken, slow-cooked into broth
content.descriptionEn: 30g Astragalus root, 1 whole chicken, slow-cooked into broth

sections.title: Daily Life Regulation
sections.content: Avoid overwork and excessive sweating. Keep warm, avoid drafts and cold. Maintain regular sleep schedule. Avoid prolonged standing to prevent sinking of middle Qi.
sections.contentEn: Avoid overwork and excessive sweating. Keep warm, avoid drafts and cold. Maintain regular sleep schedule. Avoid prolonged standing to prevent sinking of middle Qi.

content.name: Gentle exercise
content.description: Walking, yoga, qigong - stop when tired
content.descriptionEn: Walking, yoga, qigong - stop when tired

content.name: Tai Chi
content.description: Slow, gentle movements, deep breathing, tonifies Qi and nurtures spirit
content.descriptionEn: Slow, gentle movements, deep breathing, tonifies Qi and nurtures spirit

content.name: Ba Duan Jin
content.description: Especially the 'Separating Heaven and Earth' and 'Drawing the Bow' movements
content.descriptionEn: Especially the 'Separating Heaven and Earth' and 'Drawing the Bow' movements

content.name: Zusanli (ST36)
content.description: 3 cun below the knee, 1 finger-breadth lateral to the anterior tibial crest
content.descriptionEn: 3 cun below the knee, 1 finger-breadth lateral to the anterior tibial crest

content.name: Qihai (CV6)
content.description: 1.5 cun below the navel on the midline
content.descriptionEn: 1.5 cun below the navel on the midline

content.name: Guanyuan (CV4)
content.description: 3 cun below the navel on the midline, nourishes original Qi
content.descriptionEn: 3 cun below the navel on the midline, nourishes original Qi

sections.title: Emotional Regulation
sections.content: Avoid excessive worry — in TCM, 'overthinking injures the Spleen' and worsens Qi deficiency. Maintain a cheerful mood, avoid mental stress. Listen to soothing music and join group activities.
sections.contentEn: Avoid excessive worry — in TCM, 'overthinking injures the Spleen' and worsens Qi deficiency. Maintain a cheerful mood, avoid mental stress. Listen to soothing music and join group activities.

recommendations.summaryEn: Boost Qi, strengthen spleen, avoid Qi depletion, supplement and benefit Qi

recommendations.title: Yang Deficiency Constitution Regulation
recommendations.summary: Warm Yang, dispel cold, strengthen kidneys, avoid cold exposure

content.name: Eat warming Yang foods
content.description: Lamb, beef, venison, chicken, leek, walnut, chestnut, longan, ginger, cinnamon, pepper, fennel
content.descriptionEn: Lamb, beef, venison, chicken, leek, walnut, chestnut, longan, ginger, cinnamon, pepper, fennel

content.name: Avoid cold foods
content.description: Cold drinks, ice cream, watermelon, pear, bitter melon, mung beans, crab
content.descriptionEn: Cold drinks, ice cream, watermelon, pear, bitter melon, mung beans, crab

content.name: Angelica Root, Ginger & Lamb Soup
content.description: 10g Angelica root, 30g ginger, 200g lamb, stewed together
content.descriptionEn: 10g Angelica root, 30g ginger, 200g lamb, stewed together

content.name: Stir-fried Chives with Walnuts
content.description: 100g chives, 30g walnut kernels, stir-fried
content.descriptionEn: 100g chives, 30g walnut kernels, stir-fried

sections.title: Daily Life Regulation
sections.content: Keep warm, especially the abdomen, lower back, and legs. Avoid cold exposure. Get morning sunlight, especially on the back. Sleep early, avoid staying up late. Live in a warm, sunny environment.
sections.contentEn: Keep warm, especially the abdomen, lower back, and legs. Avoid cold exposure. Get morning sunlight, especially on the back. Sleep early, avoid staying up late. Live in a warm, sunny environment.

content.name: Gentle exercise
content.description: Walking, tai chi in sunshine, avoid sweating excessively
content.descriptionEn: Walking, tai chi in sunshine, avoid sweating excessively

content.name: Jogging and Walking
content.description: Jog or walk in the sunshine to absorb Yang energy
content.descriptionEn: Jog or walk in the sunshine to absorb Yang energy

content.description: Slow, gentle movements to balance Yin and Yang
content.descriptionEn: Slow, gentle movements to balance Yin and Yang

content.name: Guanyuan (CV4)
content.description: 3 cun below the navel on the midline, warms and tonifies Yang
content.descriptionEn: 3 cun below the navel on the midline, warms and tonifies Yang

content.name: Shenshu (BL23)
content.description: 1.5 cun lateral to the lower border of the 2nd lumbar vertebra, warms and tonifies kidneys
content.descriptionEn: 1.5 cun lateral to the lower border of the 2nd lumbar vertebra, warms and tonifies kidneys

content.name: Mingmen (GV4)
content.description: On the posterior midline, below the 2nd lumbar vertebra spinous process
content.descriptionEn: On the posterior midline, below the 2nd lumbar vertebra spinous process

sections.title: Emotional Regulation
sections.content: Avoid excessive worry, fear, and grief — 'fear injures the Kidneys, worry injures the Spleen.' Stay cheerful, join group activities, and maintain social connections.
sections.contentEn: Avoid excessive worry, fear, and grief — 'fear injures the Kidneys, worry injures the Spleen.' Stay cheerful, join group activities, and maintain social connections.

recommendations.title: Yin Deficiency Constitution Regulation

content.name: Eat Yin-nourishing foods

content.descriptionEn: Snow fungus, lily bulb, pear, goji berry, mulberry, black sesame, duck, pork, sea cucumber, turtle, soft-shelled turtle, water chestnut, lotus root

sections.content: Avoid staying up late; ensure adequate sleep. Live in a cool, moist environment. Avoid intense exercise and high temperatures. Stay well hydrated. Moderate sexual activity to preserve kidney essence.
sections.contentEn: Avoid staying up late; ensure adequate sleep. Live in a cool, moist environment. Avoid intense exercise and high temperatures. Stay well hydrated. Moderate sexual activity to preserve kidney essence.

sections.content: Avoid irritability and anger — 'anger injures the Liver' and emotional excess generates fire. Maintain inner calm, avoid emotional outbursts. Practice meditation or quiet sitting to calm the mind.

recommendations.title: Phlegm-Dampness Constitution Regulation
recommendations.summary: Strengthen spleen, drain dampness, resolve phlegm, lose weight and reduce fat

seasonalRecommendations.name: Spring
seasonalRecommendations.advice: In spring, Yang energy rises. Follow spring's nature of 'growth and emergence' — sleep late, rise early, stretch the body, and keep emotions free and easy. Eat mildly pungent and sweet foods such as chives, coriander, and bean sprouts. Reduce sour and astringent foods. Exercise outdoors to invigorate the body.

seasonalRecommendations.name: Summer
seasonalRecommendations.advice: In summer, Yang energy flourishes. Follow summer's nature of 'exuberance' — sleep late, rise early, embrace the sunlight, and keep the mood joyful. Eat cooling, heat-clearing foods such as watermelon, mung beans, and bitter melon. Reduce spicy and warming foods. Avoid excessive use of air conditioning.

seasonalRecommendations.name: Autumn
seasonalRecommendations.advice: In autumn, Yang energy gathers inward. Follow autumn's nature of 'harvest and collection' — sleep early, rise early. Eat Yin-nourishing, moisture-retaining foods such as pears, lily bulbs, and snow fungus. Reduce spicy and drying foods. Protect the lungs from autumn dryness.

seasonalRecommendations.name: Winter
seasonalRecommendations.advice: In winter, Yang energy is stored deep within. Follow winter's nature of 'storage and conservation' — sleep early, rise late, conserve energy. Eat warming kidney-tonifying foods such as lamb, walnuts, and chestnuts. Reduce raw and cold foods. Keep warm and avoid cold exposure.
